title: Money Value Type
description: Harden a small Money value type: parse decimal strings to exact integer cents (no float truncation), reject arithmetic across mismatched currencies, and split an amount into parts without losing or inventing cents.
starter_code_note: The class shape (integer-cents storage, slots, equality, repr) is provided in the starter. The candidate is expected to replace the float-based from_string with exact decimal parsing, add currency-mismatch guards to add/subtract, and distribute the remainder in allocate. Do NOT credit them for the integer-cents design or the equality/repr already present.
code_quality_criteria: Correctness (does it pass the tests and fix the planted traps?)
Exact decimal handling (decimal.Decimal, explicit rounding mode, no float round-trip)
Idiomatic Python (typing, clear guard clauses, divmod for the split)
architectural_criteria: Money as an integer-cents value object (why not store floats)
Choice of rounding mode and where rounding happens (parse boundary vs arithmetic)
Currency as an invariant enforced on every binary operation
Edge handling (parts < 1, more parts than cents, zero amounts)
